title: Lessingianthus dorsiventralis
taxonomy: Lessingianthus dorsiventralis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Vernonieae. The plant's classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Tribe: Vernonieae, Genus: Lessingianthus, Species: L. dorsiventralis. The tribe Vernonieae is a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Lessingianthus dorsiventralis, also known as Brazilian ironweed, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Brazil, and valued for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Lessingianthus dorsiventralis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been reported to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as alkaloids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Lessingianthus dorsiventralis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
